title: Multiple links in link column
excerpt: We add links in the Link column to external tools (Miro for workflows for example) and Google documents to give context to that specific item, but I do not want to add multiple columns whenever there are multiple links t…

posts.author: Eltjo (Eltjo Smit)
posts.cookedHtml:
We add links in the Link column to external tools (Miro for workflows for example) and Google documents to give context to that specific item, but I do not want to add multiple columns whenever there are multiple links to add.
So it would be great to be able to add multiple Links into the column. After clicking on this column cell which has multiple links in it, a dropdown could show with all the named links. Awesome!

Multiple links in Link column670×1092 51.9 KB

Related: Link-Column with a Local Link (not Web-Link)

posts.author: Concept (Alex)
posts.cookedHtml: I support this! Also needed for us

posts.author: Suzanna (Suzanna Lapchuk)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi. We also need this feature. Thanks

posts.author: christian.lee (Christian Lee)
posts.cookedHtml:
We use the file column. There is an option in that where you can add links. That is how we handle having multiple links to web pages. Our use case is background checks. We link all the court records for an individual in a single column. It ends up looking like this:

image160×619 2.94 KB

Now, I’m not sure if you use the link column specifically for any reasons, but I found this to work for us!

posts.author: Suzanna (Suzanna Lapchuk)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, looks good for us as well at list for now. Thanks. Better than nothing

posts.author: akecskes (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m on board for a better UX for supporting multiple links in a single column as well. At the very least, it would be nice for Long Text fields to (optionally, but on by default) automatically create hyperlinks out of pasted in text links (or make it rich text, so the link option is available). I’ve also considered using the Monday Doc column, since we could just put the links in one place, but that’s almost too permissive.

The file column solution is clever, but the UX is not particularly pleasant; trying to sort out which link is which and the inactive preview box is painful. Nice workaround, nonetheless.

posts.author: akecskes (Adam)
posts.cookedHtml: Ideally, it should be something supported by WorkForms as well.

posts.author: neil.kendall (Neil Kendall)
posts.cookedHtml: Podio has had this feature for ages.

posts.author: James.Deguara (James Deguara)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also like to mention this feature request as it has been directly requested by a client and the work arounds don’t look great.

posts.author: poboxkayla69 (Kayla)
posts.cookedHtml:
Eltjo Smit:

We add links in the Link column to external tools (Miro for workflows for example) and Google documents to give context to that specific item, but I do not want to add multiple columns whenever there are multiple links to add.
So it would be great to be able to add multiple Links into the column. After clicking on this column cell which has multiple links in it, a dropdown could show with all the named links. Awesome!

This absolutely needs to be added as a functionality. The update area or having multiple columns is not efficient.

posts.author: lashi (Lashi Bandara)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’ve built a Monday.com app called Local and Web Links. Its main purpose is to allow users to open both web links and local file links without leaving the Monday.com platform. It also enables adding multiple links to a single item.
Local and Web Links

The app will be available in the marketplace soon. We designed it to be highly affordable, and its simple functionality allows us to offer it at a very low price.
